summary: Reviewed the playlist-first workflow pack through the integration and architecture lens. The pack contains no diff, changed files, related files, or spec artifact, so the core question cannot be substantively verified from the supplied evidence. No implementation-level architecture regression is visible, but approval should wait until the actual spec or code changes are included.
appears_sound: No implementation diff is present, so there is no visible new client/server boundary violation, coupling increase, or schema mismatch introduced by this pack.

The documented project architecture keeps Spotify responsibilities in `lib/spotifyWeb.ts` and `app/api/spotify/*`, which is the right architectural area for a playlist-first workflow per `Project Conventions (CLAUDE.md)`.

findings.description: The requested review is for a two-phase playlist-first workflow spec and intro song URL inputs, but the pack does not include the spec or any changed files. This prevents validation of phase boundaries, data ownership, API contracts, and whether intro URL handling fits the existing Spotify/session architecture.
findings.evidence: The pack states `Changed Files (none detected)`, `Diff (no diff output)`, and has no changed file contents or related files despite the user concern naming a workflow spec.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The spec may exist outside the diff range or was omitted from the review pack by mistake.
findings.what_would_confirm: Include the workflow spec and any touched files or related architecture files such as `lib/spotifyWeb.ts`, `lib/live/types.ts`, `lib/live/storage.ts`, `app/api/spotify/*`, and any host UI entry points.

findings.description: The pack does not show where intro song URL input state is owned or normalized, so it is unclear whether the proposed workflow avoids duplicate sources of truth between localStorage session state, Supabase live sessions, PDF generation, and Spotify playlist creation. For this codebase, that boundary matters because game state is explicitly localStorage-backed and synced to Supabase while Spotify metadata is fetched separately at export time.
findings.evidence: Project conventions describe `Game State` as localStorage synced to Supabase and `PDF Export Path` as fetching Spotify metadata at export time, but no proposed contract for intro song URLs is included in the pack.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The missing spec may already define a single canonical representation and lifecycle for intro URLs.
findings.what_would_confirm: Show the spec section defining the intro URL schema, validation, persistence location, sync behavior, and consumers across host, guest, Spotify, and export flows.

empty_categories: No concrete circular dependency, layer import violation, or schema migration conflict can be identified because no changed files are present.
No client/server boundary issue is visible in the supplied diff.
No performance or scale architecture issue is visible from the supplied pack.
